title: UROUTER_SET_UST
text: UROUTER_SET_UST Change or create UST settings for the Uniface Router. operation UROUTER_SET_UST params string UST : IN ; (C20) numeric Max : IN ; (I4) numeric Maxidle : IN ; (I4) numeric Maxrequests : IN ; (I4) string Dir : IN ; (C256) string Qualifiers : IN ; (C256) endparams Parameters UST —Uniface Server Type (UST) Max —maximum number of servers allowed Maxidle —maximum idle time in seconds Maxrequests —maximum number of requests allowed Dir —working directory for servers started under this UST definition; allowed only for new USTs Qualifiers —optional string of command line switches to use in starting this UST’s servers. Observe the following restrictions: If the UST you enter matches a specific or a default UST definition, you may not use the /dir and /asn switches. Otherwise, these switches and the switches /dnp , /ex , /shr , /su , -nosu , /pri , and /drv may be used. You may not use the /max , /maxidle , and /maxreq switches; use the Maximum parameters instead. The /drv switch determines the type of server to be started, for example, ORA for an Oracle database server, FIL for a file server, or S3C for a Uniface Virtual Machine server. For a database server, the database user name and password will be determined by the first client request assigned to the server. If the /drv switch is not specified, it defaults to the ANY server. Return Values If successful, $status is set to 0. Otherwise, see the "Return Codes" section in UROUTMON . Use Allowed in all Uniface component types. Description The privileged-user operation UROUTER_SET_UST changes the settings in the Uniface Router for an existing Uniface Server Type (UST) definition or creates a new one. Only the Max , Maxidle , Maxrequests settings may be changed on a UST definition that already exists. For all maximum limits, the value -2 means no change, and -1 means unlimited.
